author: Kiara Orquera
description: Allows viewing of .heic and .heif images in Obsidian by converting them on the fly. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

releases.author: kiaraorq
releases.description:
Welcome to the initial release of **HEIC Viewer for Obsidian**! 🎉

This plugin brings seamless, on-the-fly support for `.heic` and `.heif` images directly into your Obsidian vault without needing to manually convert your Apple photos beforehand. 

### ✨ Features in this release:
* **Drag-and-Drop Support:** View `.heic` and `.heif` images just like standard JPEGs or PNGs using standard Obsidian embeds (`![[image.heic]]`).
* **Built-in Lazy Loading:** Images are only decoded when you scroll them into view. This ensures your notes open instantly and your app remains responsive, even if you have dozens of heavy photos in a single note.
* **Local & Private:** Powered entirely by WebAssembly (`heic2any`) and your device's RAM. No images are uploaded to the cloud, and no duplicate cache files clutter up your hard drive.
* **Smart Memory Management:** Images are automatically wiped from memory as soon as they render, preventing Obsidian from slowing down over time.

### 📥 How to Install
1. Download the `main.js` and `manifest.json` files attached at the bottom of this release.
2. Inside your vault, create a new folder at `.obsidian/plugins/heic-viewer`.
3. Drop both downloaded files into that folder.
4. Reload Obsidian, go to **Settings > Community Plugins**, and toggle the plugin **ON**.

*Note: Because HEIC files use heavy video compression, large images (like 12MP-48MP iPhone photos) might take a brief second to decode when scrolling into view.*
releases.name: v1.0.1 - Initial Release: Native HEIC Support
